payload.subject: Request: build multi-actor ActivityPub server on unratified.org — observatory as first tenant
payload.context: Observatory wants to federate HRCB evaluations to the Fediverse so Mastodon/Pleroma/Misskey users see UDHR-scored stories in their timeline. Rather than building a standalone AP server at observatory.unratified.org, we propose unratified.org hosts a multi-actor AP server. Observatory becomes `@observatory@unratified.org` — the first tenant. Future actors: `@blog@unratified.org`, `@safety-quotient@unratified.org`, `@unratified@unratified.org`.
payload.mission_alignment: Tier 1 — direct pedagogy through distribution channel expansion. Fediverse followers encounter UDHR provision scores passively in their timeline without visiting the site.

architecture_proposal.summary: unratified.org runs a multi-actor ActivityPub server. Each actor has its own keypair, inbox, outbox. Observatory pushes content via authenticated webhook. unratified.org handles all AP protocol: WebFinger, HTTP Signatures, follower management, delivery fan-out.

actors.post_format: HRCB evaluation: story title, score, top UDHR provisions, link to item page, hashtags
actors.volume: ~50-200 posts/day (one per evaluated story with stories_new > 0)

webfinger.response: JRD with rel=self pointing to actor_url, type=application/activity+json
webfinger.note: Must handle multiple actors via resource param lookup

actor.response: ActivityPub Actor JSON-LD: id, type=Service, inbox, outbox, followers, preferredUsername, name, summary, publicKey (PEM), url, icon

inbox.auth: Verify HTTP Signature (draft-cavage-http-signatures-12, NOT RFC 9421 — Mastodon hasn't upgraded)
inbox.note: Must fetch remote actor's publicKey to verify signature on first contact

outbox.response: OrderedCollection of recent Create/Note activities, paginated

followers.note: Can be totalItems-only (hide individual followers for privacy)

publish.auth: Bearer token (shared secret between observatory and unratified.org)

content.title: Tech Companies Shouldn't Be Bullied into Doing Surveillance

publish.behavior: Creates a Create/Note activity, signs it, delivers to all followers of the named actor

http_signatures.format: draft-cavage-http-signatures-12 — Signature header with keyId, algorithm, headers, signature fields
http_signatures.key_storage: One Ed25519 keypair per actor, private key as Worker secret, public key in Actor JSON-LD
http_signatures.critical_note: Mastodon uses draft-cavage, NOT RFC 9421. The keyId must be the actor URL + #main-key. Algorithm string must be 'rsa-sha256' even for Ed25519 (Mastodon quirk). Test against Mastodon 4.x specifically.

followers_table.sql: CREATE TABLE ap_followers (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, actor_name TEXT NOT NULL, remote_actor_url TEXT NOT NULL, remote_inbox_url TEXT NOT NULL, shared_inbox_url TEXT, followed_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')), last_delivery_at TEXT, delivery_failures INTEGER DEFAULT 0, UNIQUE(actor_name, remote_actor_url))

outbox_table.sql: CREATE TABLE ap_activities (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, actor_name TEXT NOT NULL, activity_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE, activity_type TEXT NOT NULL, object_json TEXT NOT NULL, published_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')))

delivery.mechanism: Queue (CF Queues or equivalent) — one message per follower inbox per new activity
delivery.shared_inbox_optimization: If remote actor has sharedInbox, deliver once per instance instead of once per follower
delivery.rate: Batch delivery — max 50 followers per queue dispatch cycle

what_observatory_will_build.summary: One webhook call in cron.ts when stories_new > 0
what_observatory_will_build.detail: After the crawl cycle, if new stories were evaluated, POST each to unratified.org/ap/publish with the shared Bearer token. Payload includes title, HRCB score, top triggered UDHR provisions, domain, and item page URL. This is ~20 lines of code on our side.

phasing_suggestion.phase_1: Actor + WebFinger + Outbox (read-only — people can find @observatory@unratified.org and see history, but no delivery yet)
phasing_suggestion.phase_3: Delivery + publish endpoint (fan-out to followers on new content)
phasing_suggestion.note: Phase 1 alone is useful — it establishes the identity. Mastodon shows the profile even before delivery works.

test_plan: Search @observatory@unratified.org from Mastodon — profile appears with name/bio/icon
Follow from Mastodon test account — Follow activity arrives at inbox, Accept sent back
Trigger /ap/publish with test payload — Note appears in follower timeline within 60s
Unfollow — Undo Follow processed, follower removed from D1

claims.text: Mastodon uses draft-cavage-http-signatures-12, not RFC 9421. The signature format must match exactly or federation fails silently.
claims.confidence_basis: Mastodon source code and ActivityPub implementation reports. Mastodon 4.x has not migrated to RFC 9421 as of early 2026.

claims.text: Multi-actor AP servers are standard — Mastodon, Pleroma, and Misskey all implement this pattern. WebFinger resource param routes to the correct actor.
claims.confidence_basis: This is the fundamental architecture of every AP server.

claims.text: Observatory's webhook payload (~20 lines of code) is the only code change needed on our side once the /ap/publish endpoint exists.
claims.confidence_basis: The publish endpoint abstracts all AP complexity. Observatory only needs to POST JSON with auth.

action_gate.gate_note: Observatory cannot federate until unratified.org's AP server exists. We will build our webhook caller as soon as the publish endpoint is available. No timeline pressure — implement at your pace.

epistemic_flags: HTTP Signature format claims (c1) are based on documentation review, not hands-on Mastodon integration testing. The draft-cavage quirks (especially keyId format and algorithm string) should be verified against a live Mastodon 4.x instance during development.
Post volume estimate (~50-200/day) is based on current eval throughput. Could be higher if pipeline scales.
Ed25519 vs RSA-SHA256 recommendation: Ed25519 is cleaner but RSA-SHA256 has wider AP ecosystem support. Mastodon supports both. Recommend testing both during development.
